---
title: "Indigenous Lands"
description: "First Nations reserves and modern land-claim settlement lands — the official legal boundaries"
---

```{python}
#| echo: false
import os, json
import pandas as pd
from pipeline.charts import choropleth_categorical

MAP_CONFIG = {"displaylogo": False, "scrollZoom": True,
              "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]}
geo_path = "data/geo/indigenous_lands.geojson"
```

This map shows the official legal boundaries of **First Nations reserves** and **modern
land-claim settlement lands** across Canada. Reserves are tracts of land set apart for
the use of a First Nation under the *Indian Act*; settlement lands are areas held under
the comprehensive land-claim and self-government agreements negotiated since the 1970s —
among them Nunavut's Inuit-owned lands and the Inuvialuit, Gwich'in, Sahtu, Tłı̨chǫ,
Sechelt, and Yukon First Nations settlement lands. These are the specific parcels each
agreement transfers, not entire territories — the Inuit-owned lands, for example, are
about one-fifth of Nunavut. **Use the legend to show or hide each type**, and zoom and
pan to your area — many reserves are small and appear only as you zoom in.

These are the surveyed **legal boundaries** — where each land formally is — drawn from
the federal Canada Lands Survey System. They are not traditional or treaty territories,
which are a broader concept without a single authoritative boundary and are not shown here.

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(geo_path):
    gj = json.load(open(geo_path))
    df = pd.DataFrame([{"id": f["id"], **f["properties"]} for f in gj["features"]])
    # Two display categories (the lone "First Nations land" rides with reserves; the
    # precise legal type still shows on hover). Neutral, non-valenced colours.
    df["category"] = df["category"].replace({"First Nations land": "First Nations reserve"})
    df["hover_detail"] = [f"{t} · {p}" if p else t
                          for t, p in zip(df["full_type"], df["province"])]
    COLORS = {"First Nations reserve": "#c08552", "Land-claim settlement land": "#4e8d7c"}
    fig = choropleth_categorical(gj, df, "id", "category", name_col="name",
        detail_col="hover_detail", color_map=COLORS,
        ordered=["First Nations reserve", "Land-claim settlement land"], split_legend=True,
        legend_title="Land type", center={"lat": 59, "lon": -96}, zoom=2.4,
        source_note="Source: Natural Resources Canada — Canada Lands Survey System, Aboriginal Lands of Canada Legislative Boundaries (legal boundaries).")
    fig.show(config=MAP_CONFIG)
else:
    print("Indigenous-lands data not yet available — run `python -m pipeline.build_indigenous_lands`.")
```

Reserves are concentrated in the south, where most Canadians live, but are scattered
across every province and territory; the comprehensive land-claim settlement lands cover
vast areas of the North. The boundaries reflect the current legal record and are updated
as new surveys are registered.

**A note on Haida Gwaii and Aboriginal title.** Haida Gwaii is widely understood to belong
to the Haida Nation, and in a legal sense its status is now distinct: in 2024 both British
Columbia (through the *Haida Nation Recognition Amendment Act*) and Canada (through the
"Big Tide" / *Chiix̲uujin* Haida Title Lands Agreement) recognized the Haida Nation's
**Aboriginal title over the whole archipelago** — among the first agreements of their kind
in Canada, and ones the governments note do not affect private property or local
governments. Recognized Aboriginal title is a different kind of thing from what this map
shows: it applies to an entire land base rather than a surveyed parcel, and recognized or
asserted title exists in a number of places across the country without a single
authoritative boundary to draw. This map is therefore limited to the surveyed legal
parcels — reserves and land-claim settlement lands — and does not attempt to map title or
traditional territories. The Haida Nation's reserves on Haida Gwaii do appear here, as
reserves.

## Download data

- [Indigenous lands of Canada (GeoJSON)](../data/geo/indigenous_lands.geojson) — Natural Resources Canada, Canada Lands Survey System (Aboriginal Lands of Canada Legislative Boundaries), Open Government Licence – Canada
